avalancheActivity.comment: The problem is the weak layers in the snowpack, which are deeper in the snowpack. If the load exceeds the load-bearing capacity of the deeper Weak layers, a deep and large avalanche can be triggered. There are many such weak layers, especially on axis above the forest.
avalancheActivity.highlights: Weak layers persist deep in the snowpack

snowpackStructure.comment: The snowpack freezes at night, but becomes harder during the day, especially on Sunny slopes. The crust strengthens and becomes thicker. This process will be less pronounced today than on previous days. The snow has settled and is already well consolidated, especially on the sunny slopes. Bonded snow has formed. Several weak layers of slush and faceted snow crystals are appearing deep below the surface in the old snow cover.

tendency.highlights: It will clear up on Tuesday. Faster in the high alpine regions than in the mid mountains. There will be no major warming. The danger of avalanches will remain similar.

weatherForecast.comment: Partly clear with occasional increasing cloud, especially in the Julian Alps, peaks may be foggy at times. There will also be a fair amount of clouds. Light south to south-easterly winds, turning south-westerly towards evening. Temperatures will be around 2 °C at 1500 m and -4 °C at 2500 m in the middle of the day.
